title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University President Joseph Aoun's compensation exceeded $3 million in Fiscal Year 2025.
Aoun's pay surged 113% from FY21 to FY22, mainly from deferred compensation and bonuses.
MIT President Sally Kornbluth's FY25 compensation trailed Aoun's by nearly $1 million.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
Emergency responders declared Bautista dead at the scene after a pedestrian discovered his body around 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and Massachusetts State Police are investigating the shooting, urging anyone with information to contact authorities.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in South Africa to assist 100 abandoned penguin chicks.
Major spent two weeks with SANCCOB in Cape Town, caring for critically endangered African penguins.
The New England Aquarium supports African penguin conservation through fieldwork, education, and SAFE program involvement.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras appeals a seven-game suspension for brawl involvement with Nationals player Cade Cavalli.
Contreras is emotionally affected by recent Venezuelan earthquakes, impacting his on-field behavior and causing confrontations.
Both Contreras and Cavalli have apologized publicly, hoping for resolution and improvement in sportsmanship moving forward.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez will represent the Boston Red Sox in the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman has a 2.10 ERA and 33 strikeouts in 25.2 innings, setting a record for most career strikeouts among relievers.
Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA, will play in Philadelphia, where he spent his career before joining Boston.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce comments on Jaylen Brown's trade from the Celtics to the 76ers, encouraging him to use it as motivation.
Pierce, also traded from the Celtics in 2013, expressed disappointment but not surprise at the move.
Brown criticized Celtics' management, feeling disrespected by the trade and past omissions from top NBA acknowledgments.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the Fourth of July with a parade and Declaration of Independence reading, marking the 250th anniversary.
A Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular was held on the Esplanade, starting with a drone show over the Charles River.
Performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper highlighted the Boston Pops Fireworks event attended by Governor Maura Healey.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads Red Sox with a .977 OPS and 7 home runs over the last 30 days.
Durbin's offensive improvement follows work with a new personal hitting coach, boosting his performance mid-season.
Boston retains team control over Durbin until 2031, using him to manage salary cap flexibility.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set an MLB record for most strikeouts by a relief pitcher, achieving his 1,364th strikeout against the Angels.
The record was previously held by Hoyt Wilhelm; Chapman's achievement comes after pitching 847 innings versus Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings.
Chapman, 38, celebrated with the Red Sox after securing the save in a 5-2 win, marking his 17th MLB season.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston named most collision-prone city in U.S. for second year, drivers average 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield rank among bottom cities, with averages of 5.14 and 5.18 years between collisions.
Providence also among riskiest cities, while Brownsville, TX, deemed safest with 15 years between crashes.
